title: Melbourne Fashion Week Volunteer
shortDescription: Support Melbourne Fashion Week event operations, with useful exposure to fashion, events, marketing and customer service.
description: Melbourne Fashion Week volunteers usually support on-site festival operations. Roles may include runway support, front of house, audience guidance, event assistance and backstage support. This opportunity is suitable for students who want exposure to major cultural events, fashion, brand activations and visitor-facing service environments.
hardRequirements: Role requirements depend on the official role page for the relevant year.
High availability during the event period is usually helpful.
Good communication and on-site teamwork skills are expected.
englishRequirement: Basic to good English communication skills are expected.
dateNote: Dates are based on currently collected information. Students should confirm details on the official page before applying.

title: Open House Melbourne Volunteer
location: Melbourne CBD and participating open building locations
shortDescription: Support Open House Melbourne's architecture open weekend, with strong relevance to city, design, culture and visitor experience roles.
description: Open House Melbourne volunteers help the open building weekend run smoothly. Tasks may include visitor welcome, wayfinding, visitor flow, information service, building support and roving support. This opportunity is suitable for students who want experience in public events, cultural programs, customer service and urban visitor experience.
hardRequirements: Applicants must be 18 years or older.
Applicants need to have, or be willing to obtain, a Volunteer Working with Children Check.
Applicants need to sign a Volunteer Agreement.
Zone Manager and Assistant Zone Manager roles may require an interview.
englishRequirement: Good English communication skills are expected.
dateNote: Building, Roving and Info Hub Volunteer applications close on 23 June 2026.

title: Nike Melbourne Marathon Festival Volunteer
location: Around the MCG and surrounding race areas
shortDescription: Support a major sports event on race day, with useful experience in event operations, customer service and teamwork.
description: Melbourne Marathon volunteers support race-day operations. Roles may include Course Marshal, Finish Line, MCG Information, Directional Support, Medal Distribution, Drink Station and Warm Clothing support. This opportunity is suitable for students who want to experience large-scale event delivery, sports operations and team-based volunteering.
hardRequirements: Availability on race day is required.
Some roles may start as early as 4:45am.
Volunteers are expected to finish no earlier than 2:15pm.
Roles may be randomly allocated. The organiser may consider preferences but does not guarantee them.
englishRequirement: Basic to good English communication skills are expected.
dateNote: The 2026 volunteer recruitment date has not been announced. The official page currently says recruitment will open at a later date.

title: Melbourne International Film Festival Volunteer
shortDescription: Support MIFF festival operations, with strong relevance to film, arts events and visitor-facing festival roles.
description: MIFF volunteers mainly support customer-facing roles during the festival, such as ushering, ticket scanning, queue management, customer service and audience assistance. This opportunity is suitable for students interested in cultural events, media, film festivals, event operations and customer experience.
hardRequirements: Applicants must be 18 years or older.
Applicants need to complete at least 5 shifts during the festival period.
Each shift is usually 3 to 5 hours.
English communication skills, teamwork and enthusiasm for the MIFF cinema experience are expected.
englishRequirement: Good English communication skills are expected.
dateNote: 2026 volunteer applications have closed. This can be tracked as a future reminder for the next cycle.

title: Midsumma Festival Volunteer
location: Alexandra Gardens, St Kilda, Melbourne CBD and festival venues
shortDescription: Join one of Melbourne's major LGBTQIA+ arts and culture festivals, with relevance to diversity, community engagement and events.
description: Midsumma Festival volunteers may support Midsumma Carnival, Pride March, Victoria's Pride and other festival activities. Roles may include check-in, wayfinding, accessibility support, survey and donations support, production support, site set-up and pack-down, bar and hospitality.
hardRequirements: 2027 Festival volunteer applications are expected to open around October 2026.
Applicants under 18 may need a parent or guardian to co-sign the agreement.
Some roles require a Working with Children Check.
Bar and hospitality roles require a Victorian RSA.
englishRequirement: Basic to good English communication skills are expected.
dateNote: 2027 Festival volunteer applications are expected to open around October 2026. The specific deadline has not been announced.

title: Melbourne Queer Film Festival Volunteer
location: The Capitol, Cinema Nova, Palace Cinema Kino, Melbourne Town Hall and other festival venues
shortDescription: Support a queer film festival on-site, with relevance to film, media, arts and community engagement.
description: MQFF volunteers support a major queer film festival. Common roles include Front of House Volunteers, Front of House Coordinators, Photographers and Videographers. Tasks may include welcoming guests, scanning tickets, guiding attendees, promoting the festival program and supporting venue managers.
hardRequirements: 2026 volunteer recruitment has not been formally announced.
2025 information indicated volunteers needed to be 18 years or older.
Volunteers were expected to complete at least 3 shifts during the festival period.
A valid WWCC was required in the previous cycle.
englishRequirement: Good English communication skills are expected.
dateNote: 2026 recruitment information has not been announced. The 2025 cycle opened in August and closed around late August.

title: Melbourne Writers Festival Volunteer
shortDescription: A literature and ideas festival opportunity relevant to writing, publishing, media, arts, education and public events.
description: Melbourne Writers Festival is a major literature and ideas festival in Melbourne. Volunteers usually support festival operations, venue support, visitor assistance and event support. This opportunity is suitable for students who want experience in cultural events, publishing, media, public programming and education communication.
hardRequirements: 2026 volunteer applications have closed.
Future requirements need to be confirmed when the official page updates.
englishRequirement: Good English communication skills are expected.
dateNote: The 2026 Festival has ended. The next application cycle has not been announced and is suitable for future tracking.

title: Melbourne Fringe Festival Volunteer
location: Trades Hall, Carlton; Meat Market, North Melbourne; and venues across Melbourne
shortDescription: Support Melbourne Fringe Festival operations, with relevance to performing arts, creative industries and festival delivery.
description: Melbourne Fringe volunteers usually support festival front-of-house, audience engagement, visitor support, queue management, ticket scanning, wayfinding and access support. Past roles have included Front of House Attendant, All-Rounder, Audience Engagement / Fringe Greeter and Access Champion.
hardRequirements: 2026 volunteer recruitment information should be tracked through the official eNews and social channels.
Specific role requirements depend on the official role release.
Recruitment often starts after June in preparation for the festival.
englishRequirement: Basic to good English communication skills are expected.
dateNote: Application opening and deadline dates should be marked as not confirmed until official recruitment information is released.

title: St Kilda Blues Festival Volunteer
location: St Kilda, Acland Street, Fitzroy Street and The Esplanade venues
shortDescription: A community music festival opportunity relevant to live events, creative community programs and on-site event delivery.
description: St Kilda Blues Festival is a free community music festival. Volunteers can express interest in joining the festival crew. Possible roles may include stage hand, lighting hand, set-up, clean-up, pack-down, photography, videography, merchandise sales and flyer or poster drops.
hardRequirements: The current official page is mainly an expression of interest form.
No single age, WWCC or Police Check requirement is listed for all roles.
Specific requirements need to be confirmed by the organiser.
englishRequirement: Basic English communication skills are usually helpful.
dateNote: This is currently best treated as an expression of interest opportunity.

title: Moomba Festival Volunteer
location: Melbourne CBD, Birrarung Marr and the Yarra River precinct
shortDescription: A major City of Melbourne community festival opportunity for students interested in large public event operations.
description: Moomba is a major City of Melbourne community festival. Volunteers usually support front-of-house customer service, information services, wayfinding and event support. This opportunity is suitable for students who want experience in public festivals, community events and City of Melbourne event volunteering.
hardRequirements: The current page is mainly an expression of interest page.
Specific role requirements depend on the official page for the relevant year.
City of Melbourne event volunteering information indicates Moomba Festival recruitment usually opens around December for about four weeks.
englishRequirement: Basic to good English communication skills are expected.
dateNote: The 2026 Moomba cycle has ended. The next cycle can be tracked around December.

title: City of Melbourne Event Volunteers
location: Melbourne CBD and City of Melbourne event locations
shortDescription: The official City of Melbourne volunteer entry point, including event volunteers and visitor services volunteers.
description: City of Melbourne runs an official volunteer system that includes event volunteers and visitor services volunteers. Event volunteers usually support front-of-house customer service and back-of-house event operations. This channel can be used to track recruitment cycles for major events such as Melbourne Fashion Week and Moomba Festival, as well as the Visitor Services Volunteer Program.
hardRequirements: The official page does not list one universal age, WWCC or Police Check requirement for all event roles.
Requirements depend on the specific role.
Firelight Festival and Now or Never may use internal recruitment for already onboarded volunteers.
englishRequirement: Basic to good English communication skills are expected.
dateNote: This is an opportunity channel, not a single event. Dates depend on each event or role.

title: ACMI Volunteer
shortDescription: Suitable for students interested in film, screen culture, museums, creative industries and visitor engagement.
description: ACMI volunteers may support visitor engagement, event support, administration, creative projects and special events. Visitor Experience Volunteer roles are relevant to students interested in film, screen culture, museums and creative industries.
hardRequirements: Applicants must be 18 years or older.
Applicants need to be willing to obtain a Working with Children Check.
Applicants need to sign a Volunteer Agreement.
The Visitor Experience Volunteer team may currently be at capacity.
Special events and information sessions are worth tracking.
englishRequirement: Good English communication skills are expected.
dateNote: The Visitor Experience team may be at capacity. Students can track special events and information sessions.

title: State Library Victoria Volunteer
location: State Library Victoria, 328 Swanston Street, Melbourne CBD
shortDescription: Suitable for students interested in public service, visitor support, education, history and cultural institutions.
description: State Library Victoria volunteer roles often include tour guide or visitor support responsibilities. Volunteers help visitors understand the library's history, architecture, exhibitions, events and public programs, while also supporting visitor safety and collection safety.
hardRequirements: Applicants must be at least 18 years old.
Applicants must be legally permitted to volunteer in Australia.
Applicants need to be willing to obtain a Working with Children Check.
Applicants need to sign a Volunteer Agreement before starting.
englishRequirement: Good English communication skills are expected.
dateNote: Open roles and dates depend on current opportunities.

title: Museums Victoria Volunteer
location: Melbourne Museum, Scienceworks, Immigration Museum, Royal Exhibition Building and other sites
shortDescription: Suitable for students interested in museums, education, public programs, science communication, tourism and visitor experience.
description: Museums Victoria volunteers may support front-of-house public programs and some back-of-house roles. The main value is in visitor experience, education support, museum operations and public engagement, which is relevant for students interested in cultural institutions, museums, education, tourism or public programs.
hardRequirements: Applicants usually need to be 18 years or older.
All Museums Victoria volunteers need a Victorian Working with Children Check.
A National Police Check is required and is usually paid for by Museums Victoria.
Minimum commitment is usually one shift per month, with a maximum of three shifts per week.
englishRequirement: Good English communication skills are expected.
dateNote: Open roles and timing depend on current opportunities.
source: Official website / Official volunteer program document

title: National Trust Victoria Volunteer
location: Victorian heritage sites, Melbourne metropolitan sites and National Trust properties
shortDescription: Suitable for students interested in heritage, historic buildings, museums, tourism, public events and community education.
description: National Trust Victoria volunteers support heritage, museum, events, education and conservation initiatives. The channel offers a wide range of role types, making it relevant for students interested in cultural heritage, historic buildings, museums, public events, tourism and community education.
hardRequirements: New volunteer opportunities are posted throughout the year on the Volunteer Application Portal.
Applicants can browse and apply based on their interests.
Some roles may require a WWCC and Police Check. Requirements depend on the role.
englishRequirement: Role-dependent. Visitor-facing roles usually require good English communication skills.
dateNote: Different roles open throughout the year. Dates and requirements depend on the specific role.

title: Foodbank Victoria Volunteer
location: Brooklyn Warehouse, Altona Warehouse, Ballarat Community Food Centre and off-site school programs
shortDescription: Support food relief operations, with relevance to social impact, community service, operations and teamwork.
description: Foodbank Victoria volunteers support food relief operations, including sorting, packing, warehouse support, customer service, community food markets and school breakfast club support. This is suitable for international students who want to demonstrate community service, operations experience, social impact and teamwork.
hardRequirements: Requirements differ by role.
Warehouse roles usually provide training.
Some off-site school cooking or school program roles require a WWCC.
Specific roles list their own time and shift requirements.
englishRequirement: Basic English communication skills are usually helpful.
dateNote: Specific dates, shifts and requirements depend on the role.

title: OzHarvest Melbourne Volunteer
location: Port Melbourne warehouse, occasional events, sustainability festivals and expos
shortDescription: Support food rescue, warehouse, packing and sustainability events, with relevance to food relief and community impact.
description: Most OzHarvest Melbourne volunteering is based at the Port Melbourne warehouse during weekday business hours. There may also be occasional out-of-hours engagement events such as sausage sizzles, sustainability festivals or expos. Volunteers may support food rescue, packing, events, market stalls and warehouse operations.
hardRequirements: Most shifts are during weekday business or school hours.
Shift length may range from a few hours to a full day.
Volunteers usually need to complete free online Food Safety and Manual Handling training before starting.
A National Police Check from within the last 12 months is usually required.
Some roles may require a WWCC.
englishRequirement: Basic English communication skills are usually helpful.
dateNote: Specific roles, shifts and requirements depend on current opportunities.

title: Parks Victoria Volunteering
shortDescription: Suitable for students interested in environmental protection, outdoor activities, citizen science and conservation.
description: Parks Victoria volunteer programs include corporate volunteering, citizen science, the Volunteer Track Ranger Program, the Campground Host Volunteer Program and park walk leaders. This channel fits the Sustainability category and is relevant to students interested in hands-on outdoor volunteering.
hardRequirements: Requirements depend on the specific project.
Different projects may have different time, physical, location and safety requirements.
WWCC and Police Check requirements need to be checked for each project.
englishRequirement: Basic English communication skills are usually helpful.
dateNote: Different projects are available on an ongoing basis. Dates and requirements depend on the project.

title: Green Links / Environmental Volunteering Victoria
organisation: Victorian Government / local community environmental groups
location: Melbourne, Geelong, Ballarat, local waterways and parklands
shortDescription: A channel for environmental volunteering such as tree planting, habitat restoration, waterway restoration and conservation.
description: Green Links and Victorian environmental volunteering are not single events. They represent a group of opportunities run by local community environmental groups. Volunteers may support tree and shrub planting, weed removal, habitat building, waterway restoration and parkland improvement.
hardRequirements: Many environmental volunteering projects do not require prior skills or experience.
Specific requirements depend on the project and community organisation.
Students should check individual group requirements before attending.
englishRequirement: Basic English communication skills are usually helpful.
dateNote: This is an environmental volunteering channel, not a single event. Dates depend on individual projects.
